steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that means 'when' or 'at the time'. It's a particle, so it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: when (referring to a future event that will definitely happen)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes this from just 'when' to specifically mean 'when' for something that will happen in the future. In this surah, it introduces what will happen when the sky splits open.

steps.m: to rise up, be elevated, be high

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high above us
The و at the end changes to ء (hamza) after a long vowel - this is a common spelling pattern in Arabic
Added the definite article ال which becomes ٱل at the start of words. The س gets a shadda to show it combines with the ل
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in انفَطَرَتْ (split apart). The alif is written as آ when it has hamza after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِنْ at the beginning to show something split apart on its own without someone doing it

steps.m: it (feminine) split/broke apart by itself (referring to السَّمَآءُ/the sky)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show the subject is feminine (the sky/السَّمَآءُ)

steps.m: it (feminine) split/broke apart by itself (referring to السَّمَآءُ/the sky)
steps.n: Changed the initial اِ to ٱ which is just a different way of writing the same sound

steps.m: when (introduces the time of ٱنتَثَرَتْ - scattered)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces when something happens. In this ayah, it introduces when the stars will scatter.

steps.m: and when (introduces the time of ٱنتَثَرَتْ - scattered)
steps.n: The و connects this timing with other events happening at the same time that were mentioned before
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a basic noun
Changes singular 'star' to plural form meaning 'multiple stars'
Adds ال to show we're talking about specific stars
Adds ُ to show this word is doing the action in ٱنتَثَرَتْ (scattered/dispersed)
Added short 'a' vowels to make it mean 'he scattered'

steps.m: it scattered itself (said about الْكَوَاكِبُ - the stars)
steps.n: The اِنْ prefix shows the stars did this action to themselves - they became scattered on their own

steps.m: it (feminine) scattered itself (said about الْكَوَاكِبُ - the stars)
steps.n: The تْ shows this is about a feminine thing (the stars) doing the action in the past
This is a conditional particle that introduces a future time condition. In this ayah, it introduces what will happen to the seas on the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: and when (introduces what will happen to البِحَارُ)
steps.n: The و adds the meaning of 'and' to connect this with previous events that will happen on the Day of Judgment. This word introduces what will happen to البِحَارُ (the seas).
The root relates to vastness, especially of water
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Changed vowels and added alif to make it plural - this form is used for large quantities

steps.m: the seas (subject of فُجِّرَتْ - were burst open)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject being burst open in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form to show the action was done to something rather than by something

steps.m: it (feminine) was made to burst forth (referring to البِحَارُ - the seas)
steps.n: Added ت to show this happened to a feminine subject (the seas). The seas (البِحَارُ) are treated as feminine in Arabic.
This is a time word that shows when something happens. Here it introduces when the graves will be turned upside down.

steps.m: and when (introduces the time of بُعْثِرَتْ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This و is like saying 'and' in English to connect two ideas together.
The basic meaning relates to burying or placing in a grave
Added vowel marks to form the singular noun for 'grave'
Changed vowels and added و to make it plural, showing many graves
Added ال to make it specific, referring to all graves in general
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the verb بُعْثِرَتْ (are scattered)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding damma (ُ) after first letter and kasra (ِ) after third letter

steps.m: were completely scattered (referring to الْقُبُورُ/the graves)
steps.n: Added feminine past tense marker تْ with fatha before it. This matches with الْقُبُورُ (the graves) which is feminine plural in Arabic.
Added vowels (fatha and kasra) to the root letters to make the past tense verb 'he knew'. Shows what نَفْسٌ (soul) knew in this ayah.
Added feminine marker ت with sukun to show the subject is feminine (نَفْسٌ is a feminine word)
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create a noun. This word is doing the action of knowing/learning in this ayah.
Added double-damma at the end to show this word is doing the action in the sentence.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it connects to the verb عَلِمَتْ (knew) to mean 'knew what'.
Added a small double mark (shadda) on م to make the word stronger when read. This helps connect it better to the word 'knew' that comes later.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding the shadda makes it mean 'to send/put something forward' instead of just 'going forward' - like pushing something ahead of you
Added ت at the end to match with نَفْسٌ (a feminine word) meaning 'she' instead of 'he'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Doubling of middle root letter intensifies the meaning of delaying
Converting to feminine past tense with تْ suffix. This word describes what the soul (نَفْسٌ) has delayed, connecting to عَلِمَتْ (she knew)

steps.m: and she delayed intensively/repeatedly (subject of عَلِمَتْ)
steps.n: Adding conjunction 'and' at beginning
Adding damma to show it's in nominative case
Adds the calling particle يا with an elongated alif to show emphasis in calling out
Adds ها to make it address a feminine singular subject
Root meaning 'to be social' becomes a collective noun meaning 'all humans' - like changing 'social' to 'society'
Changes from meaning all humans to one human - like changing 'humanity' to 'a human'
Adds damma (U sound) at end to show this word is the subject
Adds nominative case ending to show this is the subject being addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْإِنسٰنُ (O mankind!)
The ال (al) is added to make it 'the human/mankind' instead of just 'human'. When used with يٰٓأَيُّهَا before it, it becomes a way to say 'O mankind!'
This is a question word that asks 'what'. In this ayah, it's asking what has caused the deception regarding your Lord.
Added shadda to show the deception was strong or complete

steps.m: he thoroughly deceived/deluded you (referring to الْإِنسٰنُ - mankind)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show who was deceived. This word is asking mankind what deceived them about their Lord.
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show this is someone who does this action completely
Added كَ (ka) at the end to mean 'your' and ِ (kasra) to connect the words

steps.m: by your Lord (used as an oath in this ayah)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to make it into an oath, like saying 'I swear by your Lord'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
The ي in the middle with kasra (ِ) makes it describe someone or something with this quality

steps.m: the Most Generous (describing رَبِّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: The ٱلْ makes it specific and when used with this word for Allah, it means the ultimate or highest form of this quality. The ِ at the end shows it describes رَبِّكَ (your Lord)
This is the basic form of the word that points to someone or something, like saying 'who' or 'which' in English. In this ayah, it points to Allah who did the creating.

steps.m: The One who (refers to Allah who خَلَقَكَ created you)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ and doubling the ذ makes it point to something specific. Here it points to Allah, who is described in the words that follow as the One who created, proportioned, and balanced you.
This is the basic past tense form of the verb
Added the basic past tense vowel pattern to show this is something that happened in the past

steps.m: he created you (object of الَّذِى who)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show who was created. This word is connected to الَّذِى (who) - together they mean 'who created you'

steps.m: to make even, complete, or perfect
steps.n: This root has a weak letter ي at the end which can change form
Adding fatha vowels and shadda makes this mean 'he made something perfect' in an intensive way
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this action follows from the previous one (خَلَقَكَ - He created you)
The doubled و shows this is done thoroughly/completely

steps.m: and so He made you perfectly (follows خَلَقَكَ - He created you)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows you are the one being made perfect. This connects to the previous verb خَلَقَكَ (He created you)
Added fathas to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb

steps.m: and he balanced/made proportionate (after خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّىٰكَ)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows after Allah created and proportioned you

steps.m: and He made you balanced/proportionate (after خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّىٰكَ)
steps.n: Added كَ to show you are the one being balanced. This completes the sequence: He created you, then proportioned you, then balanced you.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows which thing (form/shape) you were put together in.
Changed the ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top. This is just a writing style change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added basic vowels to make it pronounceable
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used as genitive case
Added damma and sukun to create basic noun pattern
Adding ة creates a noun indicating one specific instance of the concept

steps.m: any form/shape (that Allah willed to assemble you in)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite and is being acted upon. This connects to مَّا شَآءَ (whatever He willed) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that asks about or refers to things (not people)
Adding the shadda (double letter mark) makes the word stronger and connects it better to the verb شَآءَ (he willed) that comes after it. Together they mean 'whatever He willed'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
Changed to hollow verb pattern with long alif, common for verbs with middle weak letter
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning more intensive
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The doubled middle letter shows this was done thoroughly and with purpose

steps.m: He formed you (referring to how Allah carefully assembled/fashioned humans)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this was done to 'you'
This is the basic form of the word used for negation
Adding the shadda makes the negation stronger

steps.m: No indeed! (responding to those who deny the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end makes it an emphatic particle that strongly rejects what comes after it. Here it's rejecting those who deny the Day of Judgment, as shown in the next ayah بَلْ تُكَذِّبُونَ بِٱلدِّينِ (But you deny the Recompense)
This word corrects or contradicts what came before. Here it follows كَلَّا (No!) to strongly emphasize the correction that follows.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Doubled the middle letter to show intensity of denial/rejection
Added تُ prefix to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word relates to بِٱلدِّينِ (the religion/judgment) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to follow a way of life, to judge

steps.n: The kasra and long ي transform the root into a noun meaning a complete system or way of life
Adding ال makes it specific. The first letter gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after ال

steps.m: in/with the religion (object of تُكَذِّبُونَ - you deny)
steps.n: Added بِ (in/with) at the start and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon. The alif in ال changes to ٱ after بِ. This word is what's being denied in the phrase 'you deny the religion'

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizing عَلَيْكُمْ لَحٰفِظِينَ - 'over you are guardians')
steps.n: This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain. Here it emphasizes that there are definitely guardians watching over people.

steps.m: and indeed (emphasizing عَلَيْكُمْ لَحٰفِظِينَ - 'over you are guardians')
steps.n: The و adds the meaning of 'and' to connect this statement about guardians with what came before it in the previous ayah
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of, upon, or against something else. Since it's a preposition, it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: upon (ready to connect to a pronoun)
steps.n: The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting to a pronoun. This is just a spelling change.

steps.m: upon you all (connected to لَحٰفِظِينَ - guardians)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all' when talking to a group. In this ayah, it's connected to لَحٰفِظِينَ (guardians) to show the guardians are watching over you all.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changes verb into active participle (doer of action)
Makes the word plural masculine
Adds emphatic laam prefix to show certainty
Changes normal 'a' sound (alif) to a long 'aa' sound (alif with line on top) and adds a small 'a' sound at the end
Added vowels (fatha and damma) to make 'he was' past tense verb meaning noble/generous
Changes verb into adjective describing someone with this quality
Changes the vowel pattern to make it plural, showing multiple noble people

steps.m: noble ones (as the object of كٰتِبِينَ)
steps.n: Adds tanween alif to show this word is the object of كٰتِبِينَ (writers/recording angels)

steps.m: to record or write something down

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show who does the writing. This describes the noble ones (كِرَامًا) mentioned in the ayah.
Makes the word plural to show multiple writers
Added َ (fatha) at the end to show it's in the accusative case
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
The يَ prefix indicates 3rd person masculine singular present tense
The ونَ ending shows this is about a group of people (they). This verb connects to تَفْعَلُونَ (you do) in the ayah, showing they know what you are doing.
This is a question word that connects 'they know' (يَعْلَمُونَ) with 'you all do' (تَفْعَلُونَ), asking about what they know about your actions
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense base form
Changed to present tense by adding تَ prefix and changing vowel pattern. This shows the action is happening now rather than in the past.
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word is connected to يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) in the ayah, showing what they know.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'if' or 'whether'
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to a word that emphasizes what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that the righteous (ٱلْأَبْرَارَ) are definitely in pleasure.
This is the basic meaning that all words from this root share
Adding the short vowels 'a' (fatha) to make it mean 'he was righteous' in the past tense
Changed to plural noun form using the pattern أَفْعَال which is commonly used for qualities and descriptions
Added ال to make it specific and َ to show it's affected by إِنَّ at the start of the ayah. This word is being emphasized by إِنَّ which means 'indeed/truly'
This is a basic preposition showing location or position
The لَ at the start adds emphasis because this word comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Together they make a very strong emphasis, like saying 'most certainly' or 'without any doubt'.

steps.m: to be blessed, comfortable, or in bliss
steps.n: This root is about comfort, pleasure and good living
The ي makes it into a noun meaning 'lots of comfort/blessing'
Shows this bliss/paradise belongs to or is possessed by the righteous ones (ٱلْأَبْرَارَ) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes that ٱلْفُجَّارَ is in جَحِيمٍ)
steps.n: This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the wicked will be in Hellfire.

steps.m: and indeed (emphasizes that ٱلْفُجَّارَ is in جَحِيمٍ)
steps.n: The و connects this statement about the wicked being in Hellfire with what came before it in the previous ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to create plural noun for people who do this action repeatedly

steps.m: the ones who habitually break boundaries
steps.n: Added definite article to specify these particular wrongdoers

steps.m: the ones who habitually break boundaries (object of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects 'the wicked' (ٱلْفُجَّارَ) with 'Hellfire' (جَحِيمٍ)
The لَ adds emphasis to show this is absolutely certain. It's like saying 'I promise' or 'without any doubt' in English. Here it emphasizes that the wicked will definitely be in Hellfire.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show something that has this quality very strongly

steps.m: an intensely burning fire (in لَفِى/truly in)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object of the preposition فِى (in) in the phrase لَفِى (truly in)
Added basic past tense pattern with fathas. The final ي changes to ى when it comes at the end.
Added يَ at the beginning to change from past tense (he burned) to present tense (he burns/is burning)
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ى changes to وْ before نَ.
Added هَا to show what is being burned (referring to Hell-fire mentioned in context)
Adds vowel marks to show this means 'on the day' or 'when'

steps.m: to judge, repay, or follow a path

steps.n: Adding the kasra and long ي creates a noun meaning the result or system of judging/following

steps.m: the judgment, the religion (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ال which becomes ٱل (with special beginning alif). The ل merges into د with a shadda because د is a sun letter

steps.m: of the judgment (part of يَوْمَ ٱلدِّينِ - Day of Judgment)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word belongs to or describes يَوْمَ (Day), forming the phrase 'Day of Judgment'
This is a negative particle that makes statements negative, like 'not' in English

Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it. This word negates the phrase after it (هُمْ عَنْهَا بِغَآئِبِينَ) which means 'they are absent from it', making the complete meaning 'and they are not absent from it'
This is a standalone pronoun that refers to a group of males. In this ayah, it's connected to the word بِغَآئِبِينَ (those absent/hidden), showing who is not absent.
This is a preposition that shows something moving away from something else
Added ها which refers to Hell (جَحِيم) mentioned earlier in the surah. This shows what they are not absent from.

steps.m: to be hidden or out of sight

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from a verb (he hid) to a noun (one who is hidden) by adding alif after first letter and kasra after it
Added ين to make it plural (more than two)

steps.m: among those who are hidden/absent (connected to هُمْ)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to mean 'among' and fatha at end because it follows عَنْ. This word describes هُمْ (they) in the phrase.
This is an interrogative particle used to ask questions
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this question with what came before it
The alif is elongated to help with pronunciation at the end of the word. This word is asking about 'the Day of Judgment' (يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ) mentioned right after it.
This is the basic meaning of knowing something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to make it causative (making someone else know). Added sukun on د to make it easier to pronounce.

steps.m: made you know (connected to وَمَآ meaning 'and what')
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show who is being made to know (you). The ٰ (alif khanjariyah) is added to show the long vowel sound. This verb is part of a question phrase starting with وَمَآ (and what).

steps.m: what (asking about يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ - the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: This is an interrogative particle used to ask questions. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase 'And what will make you know what the Day of Judgment is?', expressing wonder or emphasis about the greatness of the Day of Judgment.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و takes a sukun (ْ) because it's a weak letter.
The ُ (damma) shows this word owns or controls what comes after it. Here it creates the meaning 'Day of Judgment' with the next word الدِّينِ (the Judgment).

steps.m: to judge or repay what is due
steps.n: This root relates to judgment and payment of what is due
The kasra vowel transforms the root into a noun indicating the concept/system of judgment
Adding shadda emphasizes the meaning
Adding ال makes it specific
The kasra ending shows this word describes or belongs to يَوْمُ (Day), making it mean 'Day of Judgment'
This is a coordinating conjunction that shows sequence of events

steps.m: then/next (followed by مَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ - what made you know)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on first letter and shadda with fatha (َّ) on last letter. This word connects what came before with what comes next, showing sequence. Here it introduces a new thought that follows the previous verse.
This is the basic form of the interrogative particle used to ask questions about things or concepts
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation
Extended alif shows emphasis in pronunciation. Here it's asking about knowledge of the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: to know or be aware of something

steps.n: Basic root letters د-ر-ي became a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks َ (fatha) to make دَرَى meaning 'he knew'
Added أَ at beginning to show causing someone else to know. Changed first vowel to sukun.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end as a spelling variation of the final weak letter
Added كَ at the end to show the person being informed is 'you'
This is a question word used here to ask 'what is the Day of Judgment?' It connects what was said before with what comes after to create emphasis.
Connected the root letters together with basic vowel pattern to form the noun
Adds ُ (damma) at the end to show this word connects to 'of Judgment' after it

steps.m: to judge, repay, follow a way

steps.n: Adding the kasra changes it from a verb (to judge) into a noun (religion)
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end to show it belongs to the word يَوْمُ (Day), making it mean 'Day of Judgment'. Also changed اَل to ٱل which is how it's written in the Quran.

steps.m: to pass through time, to happen during daylight
day (a specific period from sunrise to sunset)
steps.n: Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun. The middle letter و stays as a consonant here.

steps.m: day (in accusative case - used as object or time)
steps.n: Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is being used as a time expression
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَمْلِكُ (possess/own) into 'does not possess/own'. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The ت at the start shows it's about a female subject (she)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning the essence of a person. This word is doing the action of تَمْلِكُ (possessing/owning) in this ayah.
Added double damma at the end to show this is the main doer of the action in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun. In this ayah, this word refers to any person or soul who will not have power or control over anything.
Added لِّ at the beginning to show 'for/to' and added ٍ at the end to show we're talking about any soul in general, not a specific one
The root letters were connected by adding the vowel 'fatha' (َ) and 'sukoon' (ْ) to make the noun 'thing'
Added ًا at the end to show this is receiving the action of the verb تَمْلِكُ (possess/own). The meaning becomes 'anything' because it's in a negative sentence (لَا تَمْلِكُ - does not possess).
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the command itself or the matter/affair being dealt with
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the main subject
Added 'and' (وَ) at the start of the word
Connected the root letters together. The و stays as a consonant here. This word shows when the action of تَمْلِكُ (owning/possessing) happens.
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case, which often indicates when something happens
Added ئِذٍ to point to a specific day being talked about
This word combines 'for/to' (ل) with 'God' (الله), but drops the initial ا from الله when combined

steps.m: for/to God (belongs to يَوْمَئِذٍ - on that Day)
steps.n: The word 'to God' got dots and marks to show exactly how to say it - the 'i' sound at the end shows God owns or possesses something (in this case, what happens on that Day)

steps.m: for/to God (belongs to يَوْمَئِذٍ - on that Day)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the first ل with a shadda mark. This shows strong emphasis that all authority belongs to God alone on that Day.
